article_items.title: How Businesses Are Building Specialized AI They Can Trust
article_items.description: NVIDIA Agent Toolkit - comprising models, tools, skills and a secure runtime - provides an open, modular foundation for building safer, faster, lower-cost digital AI coworkers that enterprises and developers can customize, specialize, control and trust.
article_items.excerpt: NVIDIA Agent Toolkit - comprising models, tools, skills and a secure runtime - provides an open, modular foundation for building safer, faster, lower-cost digital AI coworkers that enterprises and developers can customize, specialize, control and trust.
article_items.summary: NVIDIA Nemotron open models give teams flexibility to customize, evaluate and deploy agents for their own needs. NVIDIA Agent Toolkit provides an open, modular foundation that enables this combination. Agents Take Shape Across Industries In life sciences, agents can help researchers call domain models for protein design, virtual screening, genomics analysis and biomarker discovery. In software, cybersecurity, industrial operations and customer workflows, agents can connect to the tools and data teams already use, helping people move faster through complex workflows. To tap into these specialized agents, businesses are using a foundation they can adapt and own: one built on models they can customize, tools that connect to systems they already use and infrastructure that lets agents operate safely at scale.

article_items.title: Nvidia’s New Cooling System Cuts Data Center Water Use to Near Zero—But Not AI’s
article_items.description: The technology addresses on-site consumption, while power generation and chip manufacturing remain major contributors.
article_items.excerpt: The technology addresses on-site consumption, while power generation and chip manufacturing remain major contributors.
article_items.summary: The company recently unveiled a warm-water cooling architecture that it says can eliminate nearly all water use inside a data center, a claim Nvidia Chief Sustainability Officer Josh Parker reinforced in comments to Axios, saying that the industry’s water challenge is “largely solved.” The technology represents a meaningful engineering advance. The system delivers coolant to racks at approximately 45 C and removes it at around 55 C, temperatures high enough to allow passive heat rejection through external. The company’s claims focus on water consumed within the data center’s boundaries. Reduced water withdrawals, lower cooling energy consumption, and quieter facilities could improve both operational efficiency and sustainability metrics. For data center operators, the next phase of sustainability may therefore depend less on cooling technology alone and more on accelerating the transition to low-water, low-carbon energy sources. article_items.title: The Oversight Board says Meta needs to do more to protect regular people from sexualized deepfakes - Engadget
article_items.description: Meta's Oversight Board has made several suggestions to make reporting sexualized AI deepfakes easier and more effective for ordinary people.
article_items.excerpt: Meta's Oversight Board has made several suggestions to make reporting sexualized AI deepfakes easier and more effective for ordinary people.
article_items.summary: After the Board itself raised the issue with Meta, the company merely made the post adults-only but concluded that it did not merit removal under its community standards. It launched the investigation after it received an appeal from the user who reported an AI-generated video on Instagram showing a woman adjusting her dress, with her underwear visible in a few frames. For the particular case that started this investigation, the Board has overturned Meta's decision to leave the video up and has required the company to remove the post. Two users originally reported the video to Meta, but the company didn't remove the deepfake. If it does choose to adopt them, the Board will monitor its implementations. According to the Board's report, the reporter said they were a friend of the person being impersonated in the video without content.

The user who appealed to the Board had submitted an appeal to Meta first, but the company still didn't remove the video from Instagram.

It launched the investigation after it received an appeal from the user who reported an AI-generated video on Instagram showing a woman adjusting her dress, with her underwear visible in a few frames.
Meta told the Board that at the time the post was originally reported, it had no indications that the individual in the AI deepfake was a real person.
The person who was depicted in the AI video had already closed her account.
If the depicted individual herself had reported the video, it would have violated its Adult Sexual Exploitation policy.
The video was posted by an account claiming to be a news outlet but was actually ran by a user in the Philippines.

In mid-2025, it called the company's inability to enforce its rules consistently " incoherent and unjustifiable ." Back in March, the Board urged Meta to create a new rule for AI content that's separate from its.
This isn't the first time the Board has been critical of Meta in matters involving AI content and moderation.
